title: Quality Cost PAF Calculator
description: Calculates Prevention, Appraisal, and Failure (PAF) costs based on ISO 9001, Lean, and Six Sigma principles to identify quality cost drivers and improvement opportunities.

inputs.label: Total Sales Revenue
inputs.businessContext: Annual total sales revenue used as a baseline for cost ratio calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual total sales revenue used as a baseline for cost ratio calculations.

inputs.label: Prevention Training Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs for quality training, process planning, and preventive maintenance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs for quality training, process planning, and preventive maintenance.

inputs.label: Prevention Design/Process Control Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs for FMEA, design reviews, and process control implementation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs for FMEA, design reviews, and process control implementation.

inputs.label: Appraisal Inspection & Testing Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs for incoming, in-process, and final inspection and testing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs for incoming, in-process, and final inspection and testing.

inputs.label: Appraisal Audit & Calibration Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs for internal/external audits and equipment calibration.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs for internal/external audits and equipment calibration.

inputs.label: Internal Failure Scrap & Rework Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs of scrap, rework, and downtime due to defects before delivery.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs of scrap, rework, and downtime due to defects before delivery.

inputs.label: Internal Failure Downtime Cost
inputs.businessContext: Lost production time due to quality issues.

businessContext_i18n.en: Lost production time due to quality issues.

inputs.label: External Failure Warranty & Returns Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs for warranty claims, returns, and replacements after delivery.

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs for warranty claims, returns, and replacements after delivery.

inputs.label: External Failure Liability & Lost Sales Cost
inputs.businessContext: Costs from lawsuits, penalties, and lost future sales due to poor quality.

label_i18n.en: External Failure Liability & Lost Sales Cost

businessContext_i18n.en: Costs from lawsuits, penalties, and lost future sales due to poor quality.

inputs.label: Quality Improvement Investment (Optional)
inputs.businessContext: Additional investment in quality improvement initiatives (e.g., Six Sigma projects).

businessContext_i18n.en: Additional investment in quality improvement initiatives (e.g., Six Sigma projects).

inputs.label: Industry Type
inputs.businessContext: Industry context for benchmarking and threshold adjustments.

businessContext_i18n.en: Industry context for benchmarking and threshold adjustments.

inputs.label: Include Hidden Loss Drivers
inputs.businessContext: Toggle to include intangible costs like lost customer goodwill and overtime.

businessContext_i18n.en: Toggle to include intangible costs like lost customer goodwill and overtime.

rules.message: Prevention costs should not exceed 10% of total sales.
Appraisal costs should not exceed 15% of total sales.
Failure costs should not exceed 20% of total sales.
Total quality cost should not exceed 30% of total sales.

thresholds.alert: Failure costs exceed 10% of sales. Immediate improvement actions recommended.
Prevention costs are below 1% of sales. Increase investment in preventive activities.
Appraisal costs are high relative to sales. Consider shifting to prevention.

formulas.name: Total Internal Failure Cost
formulas.description: Sum of all internal failure costs.

formulas.name: Total External Failure Cost
formulas.description: Sum of all external failure costs.

formulas.name: Total Failure Cost
formulas.description: Sum of internal and external failure costs.

formulas.name: Quality Cost Ratio
formulas.description: Total quality cost as a percentage of sales.

formulas.name: Prevention Cost Ratio
formulas.description: Prevention cost as a percentage of sales.

formulas.name: Appraisal Cost Ratio
formulas.description: Appraisal cost as a percentage of sales.

formulas.name: Failure Cost Ratio
formulas.description: Failure cost as a percentage of sales.

formulas.name: Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ)
formulas.description: Estimated cost of poor quality including half of appraisal as non-value-added.

formulas.name: Hidden Loss Drivers Score
formulas.description: Estimated hidden costs such as lost customer goodwill, overtime, and expediting.

primary.label: Total Quality Cost
primary.description: Primary result: total cost of quality based on PAF model.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Lost Customer Goodwill
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated cost of lost future sales due to quality issues.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Overtime & Expediting
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra labor and shipping costs to compensate for defects.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Inventory (Safety Stock)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Inventory held due to quality uncertainty.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Prevention Investment
suggestedActions.description: Allocate more budget to training and process control to reduce failure costs.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduction in failure costs by 15-25% over 12 months.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Appraisal Activities
suggestedActions.description: Shift from inspection to prevention using statistical process control (SPC).
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduce appraisal costs by 10-20% without increasing failure.

suggestedActions.label: Launch Six Sigma DMAIC Project
suggestedActions.description: Target top failure cost drivers with a structured improvement project.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Average 20% reduction in targeted failure costs.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Supplier Quality
suggestedActions.description: Implement supplier certification and incoming quality audits.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total Quality Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total quality cost adjusted for data confidence (e.g., ±10% uncertainty).
